mentions.note: Managing astounding $14 trillion in assets; blowout quarter with strong inflows; rallied on earnings beat and market leadership.
Limited redemptions on flagship private credit fund treated as non-event by market; stock up 7.5%, best S&P performer; fear narrative around private credit redemptions fading.
Largest asset manager on earth, pushing new fund categories; expects a pretty good number.
Showed class act when fund gates opened — employees funded redemptions with own money. Demonstrates conviction in portfolio quality; won't short or bet against.

CEO Jack Dorsey announced 40% workforce cut (10K→6K) in favor of AI tools; stock rallied low 50s to high 60s; demonstrates AI productivity bet; may signal trend for big tech to adopt similar cost-cutting.
Private equity stocks blasted off as market detects private credit crisis may be overstated; cognoscenti believe risk is overdone.
Has high-quality private credit holdings. CEO Larry Fink told Cramer oil could be cut in half when war ends, signaling confidence in market outlook; bullish on solar as growth driver.
Private equity/brokerage rally justified as private credit chaos narrative proved false per Goldman Sachs redemption data (3.24% vs 5% allowed); buy over risky Blue Owl.
